methods.name: Peer-Median

methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Jimmy Energy is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Antoine Guyot and Mathilde Grivet that has raised €80M to date. The valuation universe consists of 8 private peers in nuclear energy (including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power) and 3 public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR).

The Peer-Median method is not applicable due to an insufficient narrow peer set. While 8 same-sector private peers exist, filtering for companies at the same stage or adjacent stage with comparable valuation tiers yields fewer than 3 datapoints, the minimum threshold required for reliable median analysis. Broadening to an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable bias given the capital intensity, regulatory complexity, and timeline-to-revenue characteristics specific to nuclear energy.

This analysis cannot proceed to a triangulated valuation range without additional methods or data inputs. To establish a defensible valuation, alternative approaches such as precedent transaction multiples (if available within the nuclear energy sector), discounted cash flow analysis with sector-specific assumptions, or direct benchmarking against the identified private and public peers would be required. The key uncertainty remains the scarcity of sufficiently comparable Series A nuclear companies, which constrains conventional peer-based approaches.
